title: Thunder Celestial Realm
content:
See : 
World
:

Overview
[
]

The 
Thunder Celestial Realm
 is one of the four major celestial realms, alongside the Wind, Rain, and Lightning Celestial Realms. Located within the Allheaven Star System, it is significantly larger and better preserved than the Rain Celestial Realm, though it has suffered considerable collapse and fragmentation over time. Once the core of celestial might, the realm housed ancient celestial palaces and immense spiritual energy, but now only remnants and ruins remain due to catastrophic battles and natural collapses. The realm remains unstable, with spatial cracks threatening further destruction at any moment.

The realm’s structure is highly fragmented, with at least 49 major pieces of land floating in the void, connected by “thunder chains”—strands of thunder that maintain cohesion among the fragments. Central to the realm is the “Imperial Capital,” once the core and closest to the Ancient Celestial Realm before the collapse. Other notable locations include thunder lakes, thunder mountains, and areas dense with origin energy, which have become hotspots for cultivation and battles. The Celestial Spirit Heaven Realm and structures such as the Thunder Celestial Temple remain, featuring vast halls and powerful restrictions to protect the realm’s secrets.

Filled with dense thunder and origin energy, the Thunder Celestial Realm is ideal for cultivating thunder origin spells. The ambient thunder is far more potent than that within most cultivators’ bodies, requiring special methods or constitutions to absorb safely. The realm contains numerous treasures, including celestial jades, origin tools, pills, spells, and at least eight intact celestial corpses, all highly coveted for their cultivation benefits.

Entry to the realm requires a 
thunder furnace
, a rare artifact usually distributed by the Thunder Celestial Temple according to strict quotas. These furnaces are nearly indestructible, though Wang Lin is recorded as the first person to crack one, permanently reducing the number of available entry spots. When the realm opens, celestial thunder descends as a guide, allowing furnace holders to enter.

The 
Thunder Celestial Temple
 is the realm’s strongest public authority, controlling access and safeguarding its power. Comprising 1,372 main halls taken directly from the realm itself, the temple is protected by a clone of the thunder spirit. Temple messengers, riding Thunder Beasts and wielding origin energy thunder spells, enforce its authority. The temple also organizes celestial title competitions, granting cultivators both status and access to powerful spells taught by Celestial Lord Qing Shui.

Throughout its history, the realm has endured repeated collapses, with spatial cracks causing widespread destruction. It has been the site of fierce battles involving life-and-death struggles, Thunder Beasts, and devastating origin energy attacks. Master Flamespark, a prominent figure, claimed most remaining fragments, leaving only smaller sections intact. The realm’s celestial gate, formed from the souls of celestials after its collapse, was taken by Wang Lin, while a massive thunder spirit and thunder lake continue to exert overwhelming power over the area.

Historically, the Thunder Celestial Realm served as a central power of the Ancient Celestial Realm, and its fall marked a major shift in the cultivation world. Its connection to ancient celestial legacies draws cultivators seeking lost treasures and spells. Despite its collapse, it remains a battleground and a symbol of power within the Allheaven Star System, often intersecting with conflicts involving the Alliance Star System.
